content.full_text:
H-52-1/20

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 •••1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

April 20, 1982


Mr. Alan Katz
2265 Westwood Blvd., Suite 144
Los Angeles, CA 90064

Dear Mr. Katz:

This is in reply to your letter dated April 15, 1982, regarding
your campaign disclosure obligations under the Political Reform
Act.

As we discussed in our telephone conversation of last week, so
long as all of your expenditures are in connection with paying
off the debts incurred in connection with your campaign, you are
not required to file campaign statements for the threshold periods,
but will report all of your receipts and expenditures for each six-
month period in the semi-annual statements due no later than July 31,
1982 and January 31, 1983. If you do make a contribution in any
amount to another candidate or committee during a threshold period
and that receipts or expenditures are $500 or more during that
threshold period you must file a statement covering that threshold
period.

Therefore, if all of your expenditures between March 18 and June 30
are solely in connection with your own campaign your next campaign
statement will be due no later than July 31, and will cover the
period March 18 through June 30. If all of your expenditures
between July 1 and December 31 are solely in connection with your
own campaign, the next statement will be due no later than January 31,
1983, and will cover the period July 1 through December 31.

Enclosed please find a fact sheet which sets out the campaign dis-
closure requirements for candidates and committees which do not
have an election in 1982. The 1982 filing schedule is on page 3
of this schedule.

Please contact me if you have any questions.

Sincerely,

Jeanne Pritchard
Political Reform Consultant
JP:cm

April 15, 1982

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Dear Ms. Pritchard,

     Thank you for taking the time to speak with me on the
phone today.  You were very helpful.  The following is the
situation I described to you.

     Friends of Alan Katz, (I.D. # 811600) filed a Form 490
covering the March 17, 1982 threshold period.  Since that
date I have withdrawn from the race and will not appear on
the primary ballot.  Nevertheless, I have received some contributions and expect to receive additional contributions
since March 17, 1982.  I am unsure what my reporting obligations
will be for the remainder of 1982 and would appreciate your
advise in this matter.

     I should add that I have made several expenditures since
March 17, 1982, primarily to pay bills for supplies and salaries
for staff.  I do not expect to make any contributions to
other campaigns, either as an individual or through my campaign
committee, until after the primary election.

     I thank you for your assistance in this matter and look
forward to hearing from you soon.

Sincerely,

Alan Katz

Alan Katz

sections.question: Since that date I have withdrawn from the race and will not appear on the primary ballot. Nevertheless, I have received some contributions and expect to receive additonal contributions since March 17, 1982. I am unsure what my reporting obligations will be for the remainder of 1982 and would appreciate your advise in this matter.
sections.conclusion: As we discussed in our telephone conversation of last week, so long as all of your expenditures are in connection with paying off the debts incurred in connection with your campaign, you are not required to file campaign statements for the threshold periods, but will report all of your receipts and expenditures for each six-month period in the semi-annual statements due no later than July 31, 1982 and January 31, 1983. If you do make a contribution in any amount to another candidate or committee during a threshold period and your receipts or expenditures are $500 or more during that threshold period you must file a statement covering that threshold period.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign disclosure reporting obligations for a candidate who has withdrawn from the race after the initial threshold period but continues to receive contributions and pay off campaign debts?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A withdrawn candidate paying off campaign debts is not required to file threshold period statements but must file semi-annual statements by July 31, 1982 and January 31, 1983. An exception exists if contributions are made to other candidates or committees and receipts/expenditures exceed $500 in a threshold period.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (April 15, 1982) and the FPPC response (April 20, 1982). Question and conclusion sections extracted from the incoming request and FPPC response respectively. Signature on response letter is illegible but identified as Jeanne Pritchard, Political Reform Consultant, based on typed name.

Nevertheless, I have received some contributions and expect to receive additonal contributions since March 17, 1982. I am unsure what my reporting obligations will be for the remainder of 1982 and would appreciate your advise in this matter.

CONCLUSION: As we discussed in our telephone conversation of last week, so long as all of your expenditures are in connection with paying off the debts incurred in connection with your campaign, you are not required to file campaign statements for the threshold periods, but will report all of your receipts and expenditures for each six-month period in the semi-annual statements due no later than July 31, 1982 and January 31, 1983. If you do make a contribution in any amount to another candidate or committee during a threshold period and your receipts or expenditures are $500 or more during that threshold period you must file a statement covering that threshold period.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Alan Katz regarding his campaign disclosure obligations after withdrawing from his 1982 campaign. The FPPC advises that so long as expenditures are solely for paying off campaign debts, semi-annual reporting is required but threshold period statements are not, with specific filing deadlines outlined.
